lesson.title: Loops
lesson.summary: Repeat code with for loops — Go's only loop keyword.

sections.heading: The for loop
sections.bodyHtml:
A loop repeats code.

Go has one loop keyword: for.

for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
    fmt.Println(i)
}

Three parts separated by ;:

  • i := 0 — start here
  • i < 5 — keep going while true
  • i++ — add 1 each round

Run it — you will see 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 on separate lines.

sections.heading: Range loop
sections.bodyHtml:
Use range to loop over items in a slice:

names := []string{"Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"}
for i, name := range names {
    fmt.Println(i, name)
}

i is the index (0, 1, 2...). name is each value.

If you don’t need the index, use _ (underscore) to ignore it:

for _, name := range names {
    fmt.Println(name)
}

sections.heading: Break and continue
sections.bodyHtml:
break — exit the loop immediately.

for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
    if i == 5 {
        break
    }
    fmt.Println(i)
}
// prints 0, 1, 2, 3, 4

continue — skip the rest of this round, go to next iteration.

for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
    if i == 2 {
        continue
    }
    fmt.Println(i)
}
// prints 0, 1, 3, 4

sections.heading: Your turn
sections.bodyHtml:
Open practice/loops/main.go. Use a for loop to print numbers 1 to 5.

Hint: start at 1, keep going while i <= 5, add 1 each time.

questions.prompt: Which condition checks if score equals 100?
How many times does the body of for i := 0; i < 3; i++ run?
What does range do in a for loop?

options.label: Walks over each element in a slice

questions.prompt: What does break do inside a loop?

options.label: Restarts the loop from the beginning

exercises.title: Loop practice
exercises.instructions: Run the program and inspect its output. Try changing the loop range.

exercises.title: FizzBuzz
exercises.instructions: Run the classic FizzBuzz program for numbers 1 to 15.
